title: -Companion Report: Affordable Housing for Artists Report

sections.recommendation:
Receive the Keeping Creativity Local: A Strategic Response to Affordable Housing for
Artists report. Should the Council wish to move forward with any of the report’s policy
recommendations, refer them to the budget process for evaluation of resource needs,
feasibility, and prioritization through the Reweighted Range Voting (RRV) process.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no direct fiscal impacts associated with receiving the report. Should the City
Council wish to pursue any of the report’s recommendations, each item would require
additional staff analysis to determine fiscal implications, staffing resources, and potential
funding sources prior to implementation.
This report provides a preliminary analysis of the recommendations.
sections.current_situation:
The Housing Advisory Commission and the Civic Arts Commission jointly developed the
“Keeping Creativity Local” report, which identifies seven strategies to improve access to
affordable housing for low-income artists and cultural workers.
Staff appreciates the commissions’ collaborative effort and focus on housing’s key role
in supporting arts and culture. This sector is important to Berkeley’s diversity and
character.
While several of these strategies align with existing City efforts to expand affordable
housing opportunities and access, they would also require new policy development,
funding commitments, and/or legislative action.
Staff recommends that the Council refer specific items for further evaluation through the
City’s established prioritization and budget processes. This will ensure appropriate
coordination with ongoing housing, zoning, and economic development initiatives.
Staff identified the following recommendations for Council to consider for each
recommendation below.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Companion Report: Affordable Housing for Artists Report CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
1. Adopt an ordinance implementing AB 812 to allow up to 10% of Below
Market Rate (BMR) units within cultural arts districts to be set aside for
low-income artists.
Refer to the budget process and City Manager for analysis. Implementation
would require review by the City Attorney and Planning Department to assess
consistency with existing inclusionary housing and fair housing laws.
2. Integrate policies to incentivize affordable housing and affordable
commercial space for artists in the San Pablo Specific Plan and Corridors
Zoning Update.
Refer to the budget process and City Manager for analysis. Staff time from
Planning and HHCS would be required to research potential policies and
integrate them into the planning process
sections.background:
In 2023, the Civic Arts Commission sought collaboration with the Housing Advisory
Commission to address the issue of housing affordability for artists. In July 2023, the
two commissions formed a joint subcommittee to develop affordable housing strategies
for artists and cultural workers, recognizing their unique role in community character
and prosperity. Over two years, the subcommittee studied ways to address the critical
need for artist housing culminating in a final report.
The Housing Advisory Commission and the Civic Arts Commission approved
recommending that the report be submitted to the City Council for its approval at their
respective July 2025 meetings. The report is included as Attachment 1.
Page 4 of 4
Companion Report: Affordable Housing for Artists Report CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with receiving this report.
The environmental sustainability and climate impacts of any recommendation will be
considered if Council chooses to advance them for further study.
sections.rationale:
Staff supports the report’s intent and acknowledges the importance of preserving the
city’s creative workforce. The staff recommendation for council to receive the report and
refer selected items for advancement to the citywide Budget and RRV process, provides
a procedural framework for Council consideration and advancement of these selected
items within the City’s established planning, fiscal, and prioritization processes.
This approach allows the City to balance support for the arts and housing with other
competing housing and community development priorities in a fiscally sound manner.
sections.alternatives:
Council could choose to adopt and refer specific recommendations from the "Keeping
Creativity Local" report at this time. However, immediate implementation of the
recommendations would require staff capacity assessment, dedicated fiscal support,
and coordination with some ongoing initiatives and reprioritization of others. Some
recommendations may require additional substantial dedicated budget allocations to
move forward.
CONTACT PERSON
Mike Uberti, Senior Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS,
(510) 981-5114
Snow Zhu, Housing Advisory Commission Secretary, (510) 981-7774
Jennifer Lovvorn, Civic Arts Commission Secretary, 510-981-7533
